name: Config file created for the first time after cyoda:setup
description: Verifies that the monitor detects .cyoda/config being created from scratch (e.g. after the user runs cyoda:setup on a fresh environment) and notifies Claude to check connection status.

input.environment: .cyoda/config does not exist before the scenario begins; the user has just completed cyoda:setup which writes the file for the first time
input.trigger: cyoda:setup writes .cyoda/config with server URL, username, and token

expectedBehavior: Monitor waits/retries until .cyoda/config appears (does not crash on missing file)
Once the file is created the monitor emits: 'Cyoda config updated — run /cyoda:status to check connection'
Claude receives the notification and surfaces it to the user
Claude suggests running /cyoda:status to verify the new connection
antiPatterns: Monitor crashes or exits with an error when .cyoda/config does not exist at startup
Monitor never detects file creation and stays silent after cyoda:setup completes
Claude is not notified when the config is written for the first time
